node.statement: (Energy constraints and Weis-Shirokov recovery). Consider m energy constraints: C = {ρ : Tr(H_j ρ) ≤ E_j, j = 1,...,m} for bounded or unbounded Hamiltonians H_j. For bounded H_j ∈ B(H)^{sa}, the main theorem directly applies: extreme points satisfy r² ≤ m + 1. For unbounded H_j (self-adjoint operators affiliated with B(H)), the generalized affine map version (Weis-Shirokov Theorem 2 with f(ρ) = Tr(H_j ρ) ∈ R ∪ {+∞}) is needed, and the same bound holds. This recovers Weis-Shirokov Theorem 3: extreme points of energy-constrained density operator sets have finite rank r ≤ √(m+1).
